preparations.preparation_text:
Put about 10 g of sample into 90 mL of SCPB and mix the sample to suspension.
Total Aerobic Microbial Count (TAMC) Test
Place 2 petri dishes and distribute 1 mL of prepared 1:10 sample solution to each petri dishes.
Pour 15-20 mL of TSA to each 2-petri dish with sample solution dispensed. Swirl the petri dishes slowly to mix sample solution with media.
Incubate at 30 - 35 ˚C for 5 days.
Perform Interim colony counting on days 2-4 and final colony counting on days 5 of incubation.
Note: It can be possible to incubate additionally 2 days. (Total 7 days)
After incubation, resulting value is calculated by averaging the values obtained by multiplying dilution rate 10 to colony number detected from each petri dish.
Total Combined Yeasts and Molds Count (TYMC) Test
Place 2 petri dishes and distribute 1 mL of prepared 1:10 sample solution to each petri dishes.
Pour 15-20 mL of SDA to each 2-petri dish with sample solution dispensed. Swirl the petri dishes slowly to mix sample solution with media.
Incubate at 20 - 25 ˚C for 7 days.
Perform Interim colony counting on days 3-5 and final colony counting on days 7 of incubation.
After incubation, resulting value is calculated by averaging the values obtained by multiplying dilution rate 10 to colony number detected from each petri dish.
Negative Control Test
Perform the TAMC test and TYMC test using diluent without sample in the same manner.

Note: Sample solution (Dilution factor 1:10) of microbial enumeration test that is the same sample preparation method can be used.
Put about 10 g of sample into 90 mL of SCPB and mix the sample to suspension.
Pre-incubation
Put 10mL of sample solution into 90 mL of TSB.
Incubate the prepared solution at 30-35 ℃ for 18-24 hours.
Selection and Subculture
Shake the container and transfer 1 mL of incubated TSB to 100 mL MCB.
Incubate at 42-44 ℃ for 24-48 hours.
Streak incubated MCB on the surface of MCA using inoculating loop.
Incubate at 30-35 ℃ for 18-72 hours.
Note: It can be possible to incubate additionally 48 hours (Total 120 hours).
After incubation, confirm the growth of colonies on the surface of MCA.
Growth of colonies indicates the possible presence of E. coli. This is confirmed by identification test, and follow the QS09004 Microbial Impact Assessment Procedure to determine whether.
Negative Control Test
Perform the E. coli test putting 10mL of SCPB into the 90mL TSB without sample in the same manner.
N-Nitrosodimethylamine (NDMA)
Note: This item is limited to outsourced testing conducted exclusively for the Korean market.
Dissolve NDMA-d6, internal standard, in methanol with a concentration of 1 μg/mL.
Dissolve NDMA standard solution in methanol to achieve a concentration of 1 μg/mL.
Mix the standard solution and internal standard solution in the amounts specified in the table below and dilute to 10 mL with dichloromethane accurately.

Accurately weigh not less than 20 tablets and finely powder. Accurately weigh equivalent to 500 mg of Metformin Hydrochloride, add 5 mL of 1 mol/L hydrochloric acid, then accurately take 50 μL of the internal standard solution and shake thoroughly to mix.
Add 5 mL of dichloromethane to this solution accurately, shake thoroughly to mix, and then centrifuge at 4,000 rpm for 10 minutes.1) Carefully take an appropriate amount of the dichloromethane layer, filter it through a 0.22 μm membrane filter, discard the first filtrate, and use the subsequent filtrate as the sample solution.
1) If necessary, carefully take an appropriate amount of the dichloromethane layer and further centrifuge at 13,000 rpm for 10 minutes.
GC-MS/MS operating condition
System suitability
The coefficient of determination (R2) of the calibration curve should be not less than 0.998.
The S/N ratio at a standard solution concentration of 1 ng/mL should be not less than 10.
The tailing factor of NDMA peak at a standard solution concentration of 5 ng/mL should be not more than 2.0.
The relative standard deviation of the NDMA peak area to the internal standard peak area for standard solution concentration of 5 ng/mL six replicate injection should be not more than 5.0 %.
Dilute 8.5 mL of concentrated Hydrochloric acid to 1000 mL with purified water and mix well.

Transfer 1 tablet to a 50 mL volumetric flask. Add approximately 35 mL of Diluent and shake the flask until tablet has disintegrated completely by using a mechanical shaker for 15 minutes. Dilute to volume with the Diluent.
Filter the solution through 0.45 μm PVDF syringe filter. Discard the first 5 mL of the filtrate. Pipette 1 mL of the subsequent filtrate into a 25 mL volumetric flask. Dilute with Diluent to volume.

Transfer 1 tablet to a 250 mL volumetric flask. Add approximately 175 mL of water and shake the flask until tablet has disintegrated completely by using a mechanical shaker for 15 minutes. Dilute to volume with water.
Filter the solution through 0.45 μm PVDF syringe filter. Discard the first 5 mL of the filtrate. Pipette 1 mL of the subsequent filtrate into a 100 mL volumetric flask. Dilute with water to volume.

preparations.section_name: Dissolution medium (0.01 mol/L Hydrochloric acid solution) preparation
preparations.solution_name: Dissolution medium (0.01 mol/L Hydrochloric acid solution)
preparations.preparation_text: Dilute 8.5 mL of concentrated Hydrochloric acid to 10000 mL with purified water.
Dilute 5.75 g of Ammonium Phosphate Monobasic to 1000 mL with purified water.
Mix buffer and acetonitrile in the ratio of 500 : 500 v/v, respectively. Add 7.2 g of Sodium dodecyl sulfate in 1000mL of Mobile phase, dissolve and mix well.

Accurately weigh and transfer about 47.2 mg of Alogliptin benzoate standard into a 100 mL volumetric flask. Dissolve in and dilute to volume with the dissolution medium and mix.
Pipette 10.0 mL of this solution into 100 mL of volumetric flask and dilute with the dissolution medium to volume and mix.

preparations.section_name: Metformin Hydrochloride standard stock solution preparation
preparations.preparation_text: Accurately weigh and transfer about 140 mg of Metformin hydrochloride standard into a 50 mL volumetric flask. Dissolve in and dilute to volume with the dissolution medium and mix.

Pipette 10 mL of Alogliptin standard stock solution and 8 mL of Metformin Hydrochloride standard stock solution into 25 mL volumetric flask. Dissolve in and dilute to volume with the dissolution medium and mix.

Place one tablet in each vessel and immediately operate the apparatus at the rate specified above. At sampling time point, withdraw 10 mL of the sample solution and filter through a membrane filter in the table below and discard more than the first 5 mL of the filtrate. Use the subsequent filtrate for the test as the sample solution.
Dissolution Parameter
Chromatographic System
Procedure
Start the analysis as described below.
Blank solution can be measured once or more for stabilization.
System Suitability
Note: Benzoic acid (the retention time is about 4 minutes), Metformin (the retention time is about 5 minutes) and Alogliptin (the retention tiem is about 7 minutes) are eluted in this order.
The % RSD for peak area of Alogliptin and Metformin for replicate injections of Standard solution and bracketing standard solution should not be more than 2.0 %.
The theoretical plate for Alogliptin and Metformin in standard is not less than 8000, 6000.
The tailing factor for Alogliptin and Metformin in standard is not more than 2.0, 2.5.
Calculation
ASAM	: Peak area of Alogliptin or Metformin from Sample solution
ASTD	: Average peak area of Alogliptin or Metformin from standard solution
WSTD	: Weight (mg) of Alogliptin benzoate or Metformin Hydrochloride standard taken for standard
P	: Content (%) for Alogliptin benzoate or Metformin Hydrochloride standard
LC	: Labeled amount of Alogliptin or Metformin hydrochloride
339.39	: Molecular weight of Alogliptin
461.51	: Molecular weight of Alogliptin benzoate
Note: Continue testing through the three levels unless the results confirm at either S1 or S2.
*Q: Quantity, the amount of dissolved active ingredient specified in the individual monograph, expressed as a percentage of the labeled content.
Chrmatogram
Blend Uniformity
Note: Except for Sample solution preparation and Calculation, others follow Assay.

Weigh and transfer whole blend sample (1X~3X: 1133.00 mg – 3399.00 mg) into a 200 mL volumetric flask. Add approximately 140 mL of extracting solvent and shake the flask until all the blend sample have disintegrated completely (usually for 30 minutes) by using a mechanical shaker. Make up to the mark with extracting solvent and mix well. Centrifuge 3000 rpm for 5 minutes.

Pipette 6 mL of resulting supernatant of the sample stock solution after centrifuge into a 20 mL volumetric flask. Add exactly 5 mL of internal standard solution for alogliptin, and then make up to the mark with extracting solvent to volume and mix well. Filter the solution through 0.45 µm membrane filter having a porosity of 0.45 μm. Discard more than the first 5 mL or more of the filtrate.

Pipette 5 mL of resulting supernatant of the sample stock solution after centrifuge into a 100 mL volumetric flask, and then make up to the mark with extracting solvent to volume and mix well. Pipette 5 mL of above solution and 5 mL of Metformin hydrochloride internal standard solution into a 50 mL volumetric flask, and then make up to the mark with extracting solvent to volume and mix well. Filter the solution through 0.45 µm Nylon membrane filter and discard more than the first 5 mL of the filtrate.

QSAM: Peak area ratio of Alogliptin or Metformin/Internal standard from Sample solution

WSAM: Weight (mg) of Sample taken for sample solution
TW: Theoretical weight of blend
P: Content (%) for Alogliptin benzoate or Metformin Hydrochloride standard
LC: Labeled amount of Alogliptin or Metformin hydrochloride
0.735: Molecular weight of Alogliptin / Molecular weight of Alogliptin benzoate

Acceptance value (AV)
Calculate the Acceptance value as follows:
Acceptance value (AV) = | M –  | + ks
The requirements for dosage uniformity are met if the acceptance value of the first 10 dosage units is less than or equal to L1 %, maximum allowed acceptance value
If the acceptance value is > L1 %, test additional 20 units and calculate the acceptance value. The requirements are met if the final acceptance value of the 30 dosage units is ≤ L1 %, and no individual content of any dosage units is less than [1-(0.01) (L2)]M nor more than [1+(0.01) (L2)]M as specified in the calculation of Acceptance Value under Content Uniformity or weight variation. Unless otherwise specified in L1 is 15.0 and L2 is 25.0.

preparations.preparation_text:
Note: Prepare in consideration of the necessary amount.
Weigh and transfer about 30 mg of p-methoxyacetophenone into a 100 mL volumetric flask, dilute to volume with diluent. (Concentration: 0.3 mg/mL)

Note: Prepare in consideration of the necessary amount.
Transfer 10 mL of Metformin hydrochloride Internal standard solution into a 100 mL volumetric flask, dilute to volume with diluent. (Concentration: 0.03 mg/mL)

Weigh and transfer about 42.5 mg of Alogliptin benzoate standard (equivalent to 31.25 mg of Alogliptin) into a 50 mL volumetric flask, dilute to volume with extracting solvent.
Transfer 10 mL of above solution into a 50 mL volumetric flask, dilute to volume with extracting solvent.
Transfer 5 mL of Alogliptin standard stock solution and transfer 5 mL of Alogliptin internalstandard solution into a 20 mL volumetric flask, dilute to volume with extracting solvent.
Weigh and transfer about 50 mg of Metformin hydrochloride standard into a 100 mL volumetric flask, dilute to volume with extracting solvent.

Transfer 5 mL of Metformin hydrochloride standard stock solution and transfer 5 mL of Metformin hydrochloride internal standard solution into a 50 mL volumetric flask, dilute to volume with extracting solvent.

Transfer 10 tablets into a 1000 mL volumetric flask, add about 700 mL of extracting solvent, shake for 30 minutes until the tablets completely disintegrate, dilute to volume with extracting solvent and then centrifuge. (3000 rpm, 5min)

Transfer 5 mL of the resulting supernatant of sample stock solution after centrifuge and transfer 5 mL of Alogliptin internal standard solution into a 20 mL volumetric flask, dilute to volume with extracting solvent.

Procedure
Note: Benzoate, p-Methoxyacetophenone, Alogliptin are eluted in that order in chromatogram of Alogliptin standard solution. Metformin, p-Methoxyacetophenone are eluted in that order in chromatogram of Metformin hydrochloride standard solution.
Note: When Alogliptin and metformin analysis are performed separately, metformin hydrochloride standard solution is repeatedly injected 5 times and a bracketing analysis is performed with the metformin hydrochloride standard solution.
Start the analysis as described below.
Blank solution can be measured once or more for stabilization.
System Suitability
The % RSD for peak area ratio of Alogliptin and Metformin for replicate injections of Standard solution and bracketing standard solution is not more than 1.0 %.

The Resolution between p-Methoxyacetophenone and Alogliptin in Alogliptin standard is not less than 2.5.
The Resolution between Metformin and p-Methoxyacetophenone in Metformin hydrochloride standard is not less than 2.5.
The tailing factor for Alogliptin in Alogliptin standard is not more than 2.0.
The tailing factor for Metformin hydrochloride in Metformin hydrochloride standard is not more than 2.5.

preparations.preparation_text:
Put about 10 g of sample into 90 mL of SCPB and mix the sample to suspension.
Total Aerobic Microbial Count (TAMC) Test
Place 2 petri dishes and distribute 1 mL of prepared 1:10 sample solution to each petri dishes.
Pour 15-20 mL of TSA to each 2-petri dish with sample solution dispensed. Swirl the petri dishes slowly to mix sample solution with media.
Incubate at 30 - 35 ˚C for 5 days.
Perform Interim colony counting on days 2-4 and final colony counting on days 5 of incubation.
Note: It can be possible to incubate additionally 2 days. (Total 7 days)
After incubation, resulting value is calculated by averaging the values obtained by multiplying dilution rate 10 to colony number detected from each petri dish.
Total Combined Yeasts and Molds Count (TYMC) Test
Place 2 petri dishes and distribute 1 mL of prepared 1:10 sample solution to each petri dishes.
Pour 15-20 mL of SDA to each 2-petri dish with sample solution dispensed. Swirl the petri dishes slowly to mix sample solution with media.
Incubate at 20 - 25 ˚C for 7 days.
Perform Interim colony counting on days 3-5 and final colony counting on days 7 of incubation.
After incubation, resulting value is calculated by averaging the values obtained by multiplying dilution rate 10 to colony number detected from each petri dish.
Negative Control Test
Perform the TAMC test and TYMC test using diluent without sample in the same manner.

Note: Sample solution (Dilution factor 1:10) of microbial enumeration test that is the same sample preparation method can be used.
Put about 10 g of sample into 90 mL of SCPB and mix the sample to suspension.
Pre-incubation
Put 10mL of sample solution into 90 mL of TSB.
Incubate the prepared solution at 30-35 ℃ for 18-24 hours.
Selection and Subculture
Shake the container and transfer 1 mL of incubated TSB to 100 mL MCB.
Incubate at 42-44 ℃ for 24-48 hours.
Streak incubated MCB on the surface of MCA using inoculating loop.
Incubate at 30-35 ℃ for 18-72 hours.
Note: It can be possible to incubate additionally 48 hours (Total 120 hours).
After incubation, confirm the growth of colonies on the surface of MCA.
Growth of colonies indicates the possible presence of E. coli. This is confirmed by identification test, and follow the QS09004 Microbial Impact Assessment Procedure to determine whether.
Negative Control Test
Perform the E. coli test putting 10mL of SCPB into the 90mL TSB without sample in the same manner.
N-Nitrosodimethylamine (NDMA)
Note: This item is limited to outsourced testing conducted exclusively for the Korean market.
Dissolve NDMA-d6, internal standard, in methanol with a concentration of 1 μg/mL.
Dissolve NDMA standard solution in methanol to achieve a concentration of 1 μg/mL.
Mix the standard solution and internal standard solution in the amounts specified in the table below and dilute to 10 mL with dichloromethane accurately.

Accurately weigh not less than 20 tablets and finely powder. Accurately weigh equivalent to 500 mg of Metformin Hydrochloride, add 5 mL of 1 mol/L hydrochloric acid, then accurately take 50 μL of the internal standard solution and shake thoroughly to mix.
Add 5 mL of dichloromethane to this solution accurately, shake thoroughly to mix, and then centrifuge at 4,000 rpm for 10 minutes.1) Carefully take an appropriate amount of the dichloromethane layer, filter it through a 0.22 μm membrane filter, discard the first filtrate, and use the subsequent filtrate as the sample solution.
If necessary, carefully take an appropriate amount of the dichloromethane layer and
further centrifuge at 13,000 rpm for 10 minutes.
GC-MS/MS operating condition
System suitability
The coefficient of determination (R2) of the calibration curve should be not less than 0.998.
The S/N ratio at a standard solution concentration of 1 ng/mL should be not less than 10.
The tailing factor of NDMA peak at a standard solution concentration of 5 ng/mL should be not more than 2.0.
The relative standard deviation of the NDMA peak area to the internal standard peak area for standard solution concentration of 5 ng/mL six replicate injection should be not more than 5.0 %.
Weigh and transfer 0.7708g of ammonium acetate in water to make 1000 mL.
Transfer 8.5 mL of Hydrochloric acid in water to make 1000 mL.

Weigh 1 tablets into a 100 mL volumetric flask, add about 60 mL of extracting solvent and then shake for 30 minutes. Dilute to volume with the diluent.

Filter the supernatant of sample stock solution through 0.45 µm PVDF membrane filter (Millipore) and discard the first 2.0 mL of the filtrate. Transfer 5.0 mL of filtered solution into a 100 mL volumetric flask, dilute to volume with diluent.

Discard the supernatant of sample stock solution and transfer 10.0 mL of 0.1 mol/L Hydrochloric acid into the centrifuge tube. Shake the centrifuge tube intensively until the precipitate is adequately dispersed.
Filter this solution through 0.45 µm PVDF membrane filter (Millipore) and discard the first 2.0 mL of the filtrate. Transfer 5.0 mL of filtered solution into a 25 mL volumetric flask, dilute to volume with 0.1 mol/L Hydrochloric acid.
Procedure

Transfer about 40 mg of Pioglitazone hydrochloride standard (Equivalent to 36 mg of Pioglitazone), accurately weighed, to a 100 mL volumetric flask. Dissolve in 10 mL methanol and dilute with Dissolution medium to volume and mix.

Test according to the dissolution test method paddle method of the Korean Pharmacopoeia dissolution. a PEAK vessel or equivalent is used for the container, and on sample is put into each dissolution tester and eluted at 50 rpm at 37 °C (± 0.5 °C), and 10 mL is taken at 15 and 30 minutes and filtered through a membrane filter. Discard the first 5 mL of this solution and use the remaining solution as the sample solution.
Note: Use PEAK Vessel or Equivalent for Dissolution.

The tailing factor for Alogliptin and Pioglitazone in standard is not more than 1.5.
Calculation
ASAM	: Peak area of Alogliptin or Pioglitazone from Sample solution
ASTD	: Average peak area of Alogliptin or Pioglitazone from standard solution
WSTD	: Weight (mg) of Alogliptin benzoate or Pioglitazone Hydrochloride standard taken for standard

1.102: Molecular weight of Pioglitazone hydrochloride / Molecular weight of Pioglitazone
Calculation of Correction factors
Note: Here “V” is sampled volume and “D” is dissolution rate.
% Alogliptin (or) Pioglitazone present in sampled volume after 1st time interval,
F1 = (D1/900) × 10(V)
Calculation of Corrected results
For 1st time interval = D1 (No correction required).
For 2nd time interval = D2 + F1
Note: Continue testing through the three levels unless the results confirm at either S1 or S2.
*Q: Quantity, the amount of dissolved active ingredient specified in the individual monograph, expressed as a percentage of the labeled content.

Total Aerobic Microbial Count (TAMC) Test
Place 2 petri dishes and transfer 1 mL of sample solution (Dilution factor: 1:50) into each petri dish.
Pour 15 ~ 20 mL of TSA to each petri dish with sample solution dispersed. Swirl the petri dishes slowly to mix sample with media.
Incubate at 30 - 35 ˚C for 5 days.
Perform interim colony counting on days 2-4 and final colony counting on days 5 of incubation.
Note: It can be possible to incubate additionally 2 days (Total 7 days).
After incubation, resulting value is calculated by averaging the values obtained by multiplying dilution rate 50 to colony number detected from each petri dish.
Total Combined Yeasts and Molds Count (TYMC) Test
Place 2 petri dishes and transfer 1 mL of sample solution(Dilution factor: 1:50) into each petri dish.
Pour 15 ~ 20 mL of SDA to each petri dish with sample solution dispersed. Swirl the petri dishes slowly to mix sample with media.
Incubate at 20 - 25 ˚C for 7 days.
Perform Interim colony counting on days 3-5 and final colony counting on days 7 of incubation.
After incubation, resulting value is calculated by averaging the values obtained by multiplying dilution rate 50 to colony number detected from each petri dish.

1.102: Molecular weight of Pioglitazone hydrochloride / Molecular weight of Pioglitazone
Calculation of Correction factors
Note: Here “V” is sampled volume and “D” is dissolution rate.
% Alogliptin (or) Pioglitazone present in sampled volume after 1st time interval,
F1 = (D1/900) × 10(V)
Calculation of Corrected results
For 1st time interval = D1 (No correction required).
For 2nd time interval = D2 + F1
Note: Continue testing through the three levels unless the results confirm at either S1 or S2.

preparations.preparation_text:
Put 4 g of Sodium hydroxide into water to 100 mL.
Sterilize using the autoclave.
Put 10 g of sample into 88 mL of diluent and add 2 mL of sterilized 1M NaOH to adjust the pH into the range 6~8. And shake it to suspension. Confirm the pH of sample solution.

Add 20 mL of the sample solution (Dilution factor 1:10) to 80 mL of diluent. And shake it to suspension.
Total Aerobic Microbial Count (TAMC) Test
Place 2 petri dishes and transfer 1 mL of sample solution (Dilution factor: 1:50) into each petri dish.
Pour 15 ~ 20 mL of TSA to each petri dish with sample solution dispersed. Swirl the petri dishes slowly to mix sample with media.
Incubate at 30 - 35 ˚C for 5 days.
Perform interim colony counting on days 2-4 and final colony counting on days 5 of incubation.
Note: It can be possible to incubate additionally 2 days (Total 7 days).
After incubation, resulting value is calculated by averaging the values obtained by multiplying dilution rate 50 to colony number detected from each petri dish.
Total Combined Yeasts and Molds Count (TYMC) Test
Place 2 petri dishes and transfer 1 mL of sample solution(Dilution factor: 1:50) into each petri dish.
Pour 15 ~ 20 mL of SDA to each petri dish with sample solution dispersed. Swirl the petri dishes slowly to mix sample with media.
Incubate at 20 - 25 ˚C for 7 days.
Perform Interim colony counting on days 3-5 and final colony counting on days 7 of incubation.
After incubation, resulting value is calculated by averaging the values obtained by multiplying dilution rate 50 to colony number detected from each petri dish.
Negative Control Test
Perform tests using diluent without sample in the same as the above procedure.
Specified Microorganisms Test: Escherichia coli (E.coli)
Media, Reagent & Diluent
Test Method
Note: Test method is selection culture method of E. coli using selective media (MCB) and confirmatory medium (MCA).
Note: Sample solution (Dilution factor 1:10) of microbial enumeration test that is the same sample solution method can be used.

Pre-incubation

Incubate at 30-35 ℃ for 18-24 hours.
Selection and Subculture
Shake the container, and transfer 1 mL of incubated TSB to 100 mL MCB.
Incubate at 42-44 ℃ for 24-48 hours.
Streak incubated MCB on the surface of MCA using inoculating loop.
Incubate at 30-35 ℃ for 18-72 hours.
Note: It can be possible to incubate additionally 48 hours (Total 120 hours).
After incubation, confirm the growth of colonies on the surface of MCA.
Growth of colonies indicates the possible presence of E.coli, This is confirmed by identification tests, and follow the QS09004 Microbial Impact Assessment Procedure to determine whether MIA is required.
Negative Control Test
Perform tests without sample in the same as the above procedure.
Mix water and TFA (2000:1).

Transfer 1.0 mL of above solution into a 10 mL volumetric flask, dilute with diluent to volume and mix.

Use the subsequent filtrate for the test as the sample solution.

Chromatographic System
Procedure
1) Blank solution can be injected twice or more for stabilization.
2) Re-standardization should be performed at least every 12 injections of the sample solution.
System Suitability
Carry over check: No peaks should be seen at the retention time of Alogliptin exceeding 1.0 % of the peak area in the standard solution-A (5th injected) when the blank solution is injected after standard solution.
% RSD of Alogliptin peak area from five replicate injections of standard solution A is not more than 2.0 %
The number of theoretical plates in standard A is not less than 6000.
Alogliptin in standard A is eluted at between 9.5 and 11.6 minutes.
The tailing factor for the Alogliptin peak in standard A is not more than 2.0.
Bracketing: The relative deviation (RDsys) for the Alogliptin peak area of standard solution-B injected before and after sample solution is not more than 2.0 %.
QSB(n) : Peak area of standard solution-B(n) injected before sample solution.
QSB(n+1) : Peak area of standard solution-B(n+1) injected after sample solution.
Standard solution check: The relative deviation (RDstd) for the Alogliptin peak area of Standard solution-A and Standard solution-B is not more than 2.0 %.
QSA : Average peak area from the five replicate injections of the standard solution-A
QSB1 : Peak area from the standard solution-B
WSA : Weight(mg) of Alogliptin benzoate standard from the standard solution-A
WSB : Weight(mg) of Alogliptin benzoate standard from the standard solution-B
Calculation
ASAM	: Peak area of Alogliptin from Sample solution
ASTD	: Average peak area of Alogliptin from a total of the 2 standard solution-B (Injected before and after sample solution).
WSTD	: Weight of Alogliptin benzoate standard taken in mg.
P	: Potency (%) of Alogliptin benzoate standard.
LC	: Labeled amount of Alogliptin (mg)
0.735	: Molecular weight of Alogliptin / Molecular weight of Alogliptin benzoate
Note: Continue testing through the three levels unless the results confirm at either S1 or S2.
*Q: Quantity, the amount of dissolved active ingredient specified in the individual monograph, expressed as a percentage of the labeled content.

preparations.preparation_text:
Put 4 g of the Sodium hydroxide into water to 100 mL.
Sterilize using the autoclave.
Test Method
Note: Perform culture detection test using pour-plate method.

Put 10 g of sample into 89 mL of diluent and mix it to homogenize. (Dilution factor 1:10)
Add 1 mL of sterilized 1M Sodium hydroxide solution to adjust pH to pH 6~8.
Add 20 mL of the sample solution (Dilution factor 1:10) to 80 mL of diluent. And shake it to homogenize.
Total Aerobic Microbial Count (TAMC) Test
Place 2 petri dishes and transfer 1 mL of sample solution (Dilution factor 1:50) into each petri dish.
Pour 15 ~ 20 mL of TSA to each petri dish with sample solution dispersed. Swirl the petri dishes slowly to mix sample with media.
Incubate at 30 - 35 ˚C for 5 days.
Perform interim colony counting on days 2-4 and final colony counting on days 5 of incubation.
Note: It can be possible to incubate additionally 2 days (Total 7 days).
After incubation, resulting value is calculated by averaging the values obtained by multiplying dilution rate 50 to colony number detected from each petri dish.
Total Combined Yeasts and Molds Count (TYMC) Test
Place 2 petri dishes and transfer 1 mL of sample solution (Dilution factor 1:50) into each petri dish.
Pour 15 ~ 20 mL of SDA to each petri dish with sample solution dispersed. Swirl the petri dishes slowly to mix sample with media.
Incubate at 20 - 25 ˚C for 7 days.
Perform Interim colony counting on days 3-5 and final colony counting on days 7 of incubation.
After incubation, resulting value is calculated by averaging the values obtained by multiplying dilution rate 50 to colony number detected from each petri dish.
Negative Control Test
Perform tests using diluent without sample in the same as the above procedure.
Specified Microorganisms Test: Escherichia coli (E. coli)
Note: In case of Specified Microorganisms Test: E.coli, only for Hongkong market.
Reagent, Diluent and Media

Put 4 g of the Sodium hydroxide into water to 100 mL.
Sterilize using the autoclave.
Test Method
Note: Test method is selection culture method of E. coli using selective media (MCB) and confirmatory medium (MCA).

Note: Sample solution (Dilution factor 1:10) of microbial enumeration test that is the same sample solution method can be used.
Put 10 g of sample into 89 mL of diluent and mix it to homogenize. (Dilution factor 1:10)
Add 1 mL of sterilized 1M Sodium hydroxide solution to adjust pH to pH 6~8.
Pre-incubation
Transfer 10 mL of sample solution into 100 mL of SCDM (0.7% polysorbate 80 + 0.1 % lecithin). After that, mix the sample to homogenize.
Incubate at 30-35 ℃ for 18-24 hours.
Selection and Subculture
Shake the container and transfer 1 mL of incubated SCDM (0.7% polysorbate 80 + 0.1 % lecithin) to 100 mL MCB.
Incubate at 42-44 ℃ for 24-48 hours.
Streak incubated MCB on the surface of MCA using inoculating loop.
Incubate at 30-35 ℃ for 18-72 hours.
Note: It can be possible to incubate additionally 48 hours (Total 120 hours).
After incubation, confirm the growth of colonies on the surface of MCA.
Growth of colonies indicates the possible presence of E. coli, this is confirmed by identification tests, and follow the QS09004 Microbial Impact Assessment Procedure to determine whether MIA is required.
Negative Control Test
Perform tests using diluent without sample in the same as the above procedure.

Weigh a quantity of the powder about 90 mg (Equivalent to 20mg of Azilsartan medoxomil) to a 100 mL glass-stoppered conical flask and add 50 mL of acetonitrile.
Sonicate the flask about 10 minutes and shake the flask for 30 minutes by using a mechanical shaker.
Filter the suspension through 0.45 µm PVDF syringe filter, Discard the first 5 mL of the filtrate, Pipet 5 mL of the subsequent filtrate into a 100 mL volumetric flask and dilute with acetonitrile to volume.
Procedure

Note: Sampling of Azilsartan medoxomil potassium standard should be carried out in the controlled humidity glove box that is controlled below 20 % RH.
Accurately weigh about 43 mg of Azilsartan medoxomil potassium standard (Equivalent to 40 mg of Azilsartan medoxomil) into a 50 mL volumetric flask, dilute with Acetonitrile to volume and mix.
Accurately weigh about 25 mg of Chlorthalidone standard into a 50 mL volumetric flask, dilute with Acetonitrile to volume and mix.

Add exactly 5 mL of the Internal standard solution and dilute with acetonitrile to volume and mix.

Filter the solution through a 0.45 μm membrane filter (Millipore, 0.45 μm PVDF syringe filter and 0.45 μm Nylon syringe filter).
Discard the first 5 mL or more of the filtrate and pipet 5 mL of the subsequent filtrate into a 25 mL of volumetric flask. Pipette 5 mL of internal standard solution and make up mark with diluent.

Transfer 10 tablets in to a 500 mL volumetric flask and add 300 mL of Diluent. Shake the flask until all the tablets have completely disintegrated (usually for 20 minutes).
Sonicate for 5 minutes and dilute to volume with Diluent. Filter the solution through a membrane filter having a porosity of about 0.45 μm in the table below.
Discard the first 5 mL or more of the filtrate and pipet 5 mL of the subsequent filtrate into a 25 mL of volumetric flask. Pipette 5 mL of internal standard solution into above volumetric flask and dilute to volume with Diluent.

Start the analysis as described below.

The relative standard deviation of the peak area ratios (Azilsartan medoxomil/Internal standard) from replicate injections of standard solution and bracketing standard solution is not more than 1.0 %.
The symmetry factor for the Azilsartan medoxomil peak in standard solution peak is not more than 1.5.

The relative standard deviation of the peak area ratios (Chlorthalidone /Internal standard) from replicate injections of standard solution and bracketing standard solution is not more than 1.0 %.

preparations.preparation_text: Put 10 g of sample into 90 mL of diluent and mix it to homogenize. (Dilution factor 1:10)

Take 20mL of the sample solution (Dilution factor 1:10) and transfer in to 80mL of diluent and mix it to homogenize.
Total Aerobic Microbial Count (TAMC) Test
Place 2 petri dishes and transfer 1 mL of sample solution (Dilution factor 1:50) into each petri dish.
Pour 15 ~ 20 mL of TSA to each petri dish with sample solution dispersed. Swirl the petri dishes slowly to mix sample with media.
Incubate at 30 - 35 ˚C for 5 days.
Perform interim colony counting on days 2-4 and final colony counting on days 5 of incubation.
Note: It can be possible to incubate additionally 2 days (Total 7 days).
After incubation, resulting value is calculated by averaging the values obtained by multiplying dilution rate 50 to colony number detected from each petri dish.
Total Combined Yeasts and Molds Count (TYMC) Test
Place 2 petri dishes and transfer 1 mL of sample solution (Dilution factor 1:50) into each petri dish.
Pour 15 ~ 20 mL of SDA to each petri dish with sample solution dispersed. Swirl the petri dishes slowly to mix sample with media.
Incubate at 20 - 25 ˚C for 7 days.
Perform Interim colony counting on days 3-5 and final colony counting on days 7 of incubation.
After incubation, resulting value is calculated by averaging the values obtained by multiplying dilution rate 50 to colony number detected from each petri dish.
Negative Control Test
Perform tests using diluent without sample in the same as the above procedure.
Specified Microorganisms Test: Escherichia coli (E. coli)
Note: In case of Specified Microorganisms Test: E.coli, only for Hongkong market.
Reagent, Diluent and Media
Test Method
Note: Test method is selection culture method of E. coli using selective media (MCB) and confirmatory medium (MCA).

Note: Sample solution (Dilution factor 1:10) of microbial enumeration test that is the same sample solution method can be used.
Put 10 g of sample into 90 mL of diluent and mix it to homogenize. (Dilution factor 1:10)
Pre-incubation
Transfer 10 mL of sample solution into 100 mL of SCDM (0.7% polysorbate 80 + 0.1 % lecithin). After that, mix the sample to homogenize.
Incubate at 30 - 35 ℃ for 18 - 24 hours.
Selection and Subculture
Shake the container and transfer 1 mL of incubated SCDM (0.7% polysorbate 80 + 0.1 % lecithin) to 100 mL MCB.
Incubate at 42 - 44 ℃ for 24 - 48 hours.
Streak incubated MCB on the surface of MCA using inoculating loop.
Incubate at 30 - 35 ℃ for 18 - 72 hours.
Note: It can be possible to incubate additionally 48 hours (Total 120 hours).
After incubation, confirm the growth of colonies on the surface of MCA.
Growth of colonies indicates the possible presence of E. coli, this is confirmed by identification tests, and follow the QS09004 Microbial Impact Assessment Procedure to determine whether MIA is required.
Negative Control Test
Perform tests using diluent without sample in the same as the above procedure.

Note: Chlorthalidone, Azilsartan medoxomil and p-methylbenzophenone are eluted in that order.
Azilsartan medoxomil (260 nm)
The relative standard deviation of the peak area ratios (Azilsartan medoxomil/Internal standard) from replicate injections of standard solution is not more than 1.0 %.
The relative standard deviation of the peak area ratios (Azilsartan medoxomil/Internal standard) from replicate injections of standard solution and bracketing standard solution is not more than 1.0 %.
The symmetry factor for the Azilsartan medoxomil peak in standard solution peak is not more than 1.5.
The resolution between Azilsartan medoxomil and p-methylbenzophenone in standard solution is not less than 5.
Chlorthalidone (228nm)
The relative standard deviation of the peak area ratios (Chlorthalidone /Internal standard) from replicate injections of standard solution is not more than 1.0 %.
The relative standard deviation of the peak area ratios (Chlorthalidone /Internal standard) from replicate injections of standard solution and bracketing standard solution is not more than 1.0 %.
The symmetry factor for the Chlorthalidone peak in standard solution peak is not more than 1.5.
Calculation
At	: Peak area ratio (Azilsartan medoxomil or Chlorthalidone/internal standard) from the sample solution
AS    : Average peak area ratio (Azilsartan medoxomil or Chlorthalidone/internal standard) from the standard solution
WS	: Weight of Azilsartan medoxomil potassium or Chlorthalidone standard (mg)
P	: Potency of Azilsartan medoxomil potassium or Chlorthalidone standard (%)
LC	: Azilsartan Medoxomil or Chlorthalidone Labeled Claim (mg)
0.937	: Molecular Weight Conversion Factor (Azilsartan Medoxomil/Azilsartan
Medoxomil Potassium)
TW	: Theoretical weight of the blend for L in mg.
WT 	: Weight of sample taken in mg
Chrmatogram
The peak detected at 17 minutes is the internal standard solution peak.

preparations.preparation_text:
Azilsartan medoxomil potassium is heat stressed until at least 0.01 % is detected for identification of degradation substances(D-1). (Typically, 1 day at 80 °C)
Transfer about 17 mg of the heat stressed Azilsartan medoxomil potassium into a 10 mL volumetric flask. Dissolve in and dilute with the acetonitrile to volume and mix (Concentration: 1.7 mg/mL)
Weigh and transfer about 5 mg of Chlorthalidone standard, into a 10 mL volumetric flask. Dissolve in and dilute with acetonitrile to volume. (Concentration: 500 μg/mL)

Transfer 10 tablets into a 250 mL volumetric flask, add about 150 mL of diluent. Stir the flask until the tablets completely disintegrate (Use the mechanical stirrer if necessary), sonicate for 5 minutes and then dilute to volume with diluent.
Filter this solution through about 0.45 µm membrane filter in the table below and discard the first 5 mL of the filtrate and use the subsequent filtrate.

Transfer 10 tablets into a 500 mL volumetric flask, add about 300 mL of diluent. Stir the flask until the tablets completely disintegrate (Use the mechanical stirrer if necessary), sonicate for 5 minutes and then dilute to volume with diluent.
Filter this solution through about 0.45 µm membrane filter in the table below and discard the first 5 mL of the filtrate and use the subsequent filtrate.
Chromatographic system
Procedure
Start the analysis as described below.
Limit of Quantitation and Limit of Detection
The LOQ applies to both known and unknown related substances.
Relative retention time and Correction factor information (Azilsartan medoxomil)
Relative retention time and Correction factor information (Chlorthalidone)
C-1 is a tautomer of chlorthalidone and is not a degradation substance, so it is excluded from the calculation of related substances.
It was calculated based on the azilsartan medoxomil peak retention time.
System suitability
Note: C-1, Chlorthalidone, U-6 and Azlisartan medoxomil are eluted in that order from resolution solution.
The relative standard deviation of the peak response of Azilsartan medoxomil and Chlorthalidone from replicate injections of standard solution are not more than 5.7 %.
The relative standard deviation of the peak response of Azilsartan medoxomil and Chlorthalidone from replicate injections of standard solution and bracketing injection of standard solution are not more than 5.7 %.
The tailing factors of Azilsartan medoxomil and Chlorthalidone from standard solution are not more than 1.5
The resolution between C-1 and Chlorthalidone from resolution solution is not less than 2.0.
The resolution between U-6 and Azilsartan medoxomil from resolution solution is not less than 3.5.
The peak area ratio of the Quantitation limit solution and standard solution is 40 to 60.
Calculation
Note: Peak of RRT 0.04(placebo peak) is not integrated.
Note: Exclude any peaks due to the blank from calculation.
AT	: Peak area of each impurity from the sample solution
AS	: Average peak area of Azilsartan medoxomil or Chlorthalidone from the standard solution
WS	: Weight of Azilsartan medoxomil potassium or Chlorthalidone standard (mg)
P	: Potency of Azilsartan medoxomil potassium or Chlorthalidone standard (%)
LC	: Azilsartan Medoxomil or Chlorthalidone Labeled Claim (mg)
0.937	: Molecular Weight Conversion of Azilsartan Medoxomil/Azilsartan
Medoxomil Potassium
F	: Correction factor

preparations.preparation_text: Put 10 g of sample into 90 mL of diluent and mix it to homogenize. (Dilution factor 1:10)

Take 20mL of the sample solution (Dilution factor 1:10) and transfer in to 80mL of diluent and mix it to homogenize.
Total Aerobic Microbial Count (TAMC) Test
Place 2 petri dishes and transfer 1 mL of sample solution (Dilution factor 1:50) into each petri dish.
Pour 15 ~ 20 mL of TSA to each petri dish with sample solution dispersed. Swirl the petri dishes slowly to mix sample with media.
Incubate at 30 - 35 ˚C for 5 days.
Perform interim colony counting on days 2-4 and final colony counting on days 5 of incubation.
Note: It can be possible to incubate additionally 2 days (Total 7 days).
After incubation, resulting value is calculated by averaging the values obtained by multiplying dilution rate 50 to colony number detected from each petri dish.
Total Combined Yeasts and Molds Count (TYMC) Test
Place 2 petri dishes and transfer 1 mL of sample solution (Dilution factor 1:50) into each petri dish.
Pour 15 ~ 20 mL of SDA to each petri dish with sample solution dispersed. Swirl the petri dishes slowly to mix sample with media.
Incubate at 20 - 25 ˚C for 7 days.
Perform Interim colony counting on days 3-5 and final colony counting on days 7 of incubation.
After incubation, resulting value is calculated by averaging the values obtained by multiplying dilution rate 50 to colony number detected from each petri dish.
Negative Control Test
Perform tests using diluent without sample in the same as the above procedure.
Specified Microorganisms Test: Escherichia coli (E. coli)
Note: In case of Specified Microorganisms Test: E.coli, only for Hongkong market.
Reagent, Diluent and Media
Test Method
Note: Test method is selection culture method of E. coli using selective media (MCB) and confirmatory medium (MCA).

Note: Sample solution (Dilution factor 1:10) of microbial enumeration test that is the same sample solution method can be used.
Put 10 g of sample into 90 mL of diluent and mix it to homogenize. (Dilution factor 1:10)
Pre-incubation
Transfer 10 mL of sample solution into 100 mL of SCDM (0.7% polysorbate 80 + 0.1 % lecithin). After that, mix the sample to homogenize.
Incubate at 30 - 35 ℃ for 18 - 24 hours.
Selection and Subculture
Shake the container and transfer 1 mL of incubated SCDM (0.7% polysorbate 80 + 0.1 % lecithin) to 100 mL MCB.
Incubate at 42 - 44 ℃ for 24 - 48 hours.
Streak incubated MCB on the surface of MCA using inoculating loop.
Incubate at 30 - 35 ℃ for 18 - 72 hours.
Note: It can be possible to incubate additionally 48 hours (Total 120 hours).
After incubation, confirm the growth of colonies on the surface of MCA.
Growth of colonies indicates the possible presence of E. coli, this is confirmed by identification tests, and follow the QS09004 Microbial Impact Assessment Procedure to determine whether MIA is required.
Negative Control Test
Perform tests using diluent without sample in the same as the above procedure.

In case the identification test is performed together with the Assay:
Proceed as directed under the test “Assay”.
In case only the identification test is performed:
Transfer 1 tablet into a 50 mL volumetric flask (Do not grind tablet). Add 30 mL of diluent (3) and sonicate for 20 minutes with intermediate shaking (at least every 5 minutes and check whether the tablet is completely disintegrated). Then dilute to volume with diluent (3) and mix well.
Centrifuge the sample solution at 4500 rpm for 10 minutes or filter the sample solution through 0.45 µm PVDF (Hyundai) or 0.45 µm Nylon (Hyundai) syringe filter.
Pipette 4 mL of above solution into 20 mL volumetric flask and dilute to volume with diluent (3) and mix well.

The relative standard deviation for peak areas of Amlodipine from the repeated injection of standard and injection of bracketing should not be more than 2.0 %.
Calculation
Note: Continue testing through the three levels unless the results confirm at either S1 or S2.

Mix Milli-Q water, acetonitrile and acetic acid in the ratio of 20: 80: 2 v/v, respectively.
Mix Milli-Q water, acetonitrile and acetic acid in the ratio of 30: 70: 2 v/v, respectively.
Mix 3 mL of dissolution medium and 3 mL of Diluent-B in a Falcon tube and mix well.

% Release of Azilsartan medoxomil
Calculation of Correction factors
Note: Here “V” is sampled volume and “D” is dissolution rate.
% Azilsartan medxomil present in sampled volume after 1st time interval,
F1 = (D1/900) × 10(V)
Calculation of Corrected results
For 1st time interval = D1 (No correction required).
For 2nd time interval = D2 + F1

Acceptance value (AV)
Calculate the Acceptance value as follows:
Acceptance value (AV) = | M –  | + ks
The requirements for dosage uniformity are met if the acceptance value of the first 10 dosage units is less than or equal to L1 %, maximum allowed acceptance value.
If the acceptance value is > L1, test additional 20 units and calculate the acceptance value. The requirements are met if the final acceptance value of the 30 dosage units is ≤ L1, and no individual content of any dosage units is less than [1-(0.01)(L2)]M nor more than [1+(0.01)(L2)]M as specified in the calculation of Acceptance Value under Content Uniformity or weight variation. 물과 아세토니트릴을 각각 10:90 v/v의 비율로 혼합한다.

아질사르탄 표준품 약 57.7 mg을 달아 50 mL 용량플라스크에 넣는다.
희석액 30 mL를 추가하고 5분간 초음파 처리하여 녹이고 희석액으로 표선한다.

암로디핀베실산염 표준품 약 30.8 mg을 달아 100 mL 용량플라스크에 넣는다.
희석액 70 mL를 추가하고 5분간 초음파 처리하여 녹이고 희석액으로 표선한다.
아질사르탄 표준원액 5 mL 및 암로디핀 표준원액 5 mL 및 아세토니트릴 11 mL를 200 mL 용량플라스크에 넣고 시험액으로 표선하고 잘 섞는다.
아질사르탄 표준원액 5 mL 및 암로디핀 표준원액 10 mL 및 아세토니트릴 6 mL를 200 mL 용량플라스크에 넣고 시험액으로 표선하고 잘 섞는다.
아질사르탄 표준원액 10 mL 및 암로디핀 표준원액 5 mL 및 아세토니트릴 6 mL를 200 mL 용량플라스크에 넣고 시험액으로 표선하고 잘 섞는다.
아질사르탄 표준원액 5 mL 및 암로디핀 표준원액 5 mL 및 아세토니트릴 1 mL를 100 mL 용량플라스크에 넣고 시험액으로 표선하고 잘 섞는다.

시험액 900 mL을 각 용출 용기에 옮긴다. 각 vessel에 하나의 정제를 넣고 용출 파라미터에 따라 용출을 수행한다.
정해진 시간에 따라 각 vessel에서 충분한 양의 검액을 10 µm Auto sampler filter로 샘플링하여 검액으로 하거나 각 vessel에서 충분한 양의 검액을 샘플링하고 0.45 µm PVDF syringe filter로 여과하여 검액으로 한다.

희석액-B 56 mL를 넣고 최소 5분마다 흔들어주며 15분간 초음파 처리한다. 희석액-A로 표선하고 잘 섞는다.
위 용액 5 mL를 취하여 20 mL 용량플라스크에 넣고 희석액-A로 표선하고 잘 섞는다.
검액을 4500 rpm으로 10 분간 원심분리 하거나 0.45 µm PTFE 시린지 필터로 여과한다.

판정값 (AV)
다음 식에 따라 판정값을 계산한다.
판정값 (AV) = | M –  | + ks
처음 10 개 검체의 판정값이 판정값의 최대 허용한도L1 % 와 같거나 미만일 경우, 제제 균일성 조건을 충족한다.
만약 판정값이 L1 %를 넘을 경우, 추가 검체 20 개를 가지고 같은 방법으로 시험하여 판정값을 계산한다. 30 개 검체의 최종 판정값이 L1 % 이하인 경우, 개개 제제의 함량이 함량 균일성 또는 질량편차의 판정값 계산 항에 표시된 [1-(0.01)(L2)]M 이상이며 또한 [1+(0.01)(L2)]M을 초과하는 것이 없을 때 조건을 충족한다. 따로 규정이 없는 한, L1은 15.0, L2는 25.0 이다.
Ammonium phosphate monobasic 약 3.45 g을 적당한 용기에 옮기고, 물 1000 mL에 녹이고 잘 섞는다. ortho-phosphoric acid로 pH가 2.0±0.02 되도록 조정한다.
완충액, 메탄올 및 아세토니트릴을 600 : 250 : 150 v/v/v의 비율로 혼합한다. 10분간 탈기한다.
완충액, 메탄올 및 아세토니트릴을 300 : 100 : 600 v/v/v의 비율로 혼합한다. 10분간 탈기한다.
물, 아세토니트릴 및 메탄올을 30 : 35 : 35 v/v/v의 비율로 혼합한다.
아세토니트릴 및 메탄올을 50 : 50 v/v의 비율로 혼합한다.

preparations.preparation_text:
Ammonium phosphate monobasic (Sigma-Aldrich) 약 2.30 g을 달아 물 1000 mL에 넣는다.
ortho-phosphoric acid로 pH가 2.0±0.02 되도록 조정한다.

완충액 (20 mM NH4H2PO4), 메탄올 및 아세토니트릴을 14 : 3 : 3 v/v/v의 비율로 혼합한다.
Note: 이동상을 멤브레인 필터로 여과하고 탈기한다.

완충액 (20 mM NH4H2PO4), 메탄올 및 아세토니트릴을 30 : 10 : 60 v/v/v의 비율로 혼합한다.
Note: 이동상을 멤브레인 필터로 여과하고 탈기한다.
물, 메탄올 및 아세토니트릴을 20 : 40 : 40 v/v/v의 비율로 혼합한다.

아질사르탄 표준품 약 22 mg을 정밀하게 달아 100 mL 용량플라스크에 넣는다.
희석액으로 녹이고 표선한 후 잘 섞는다. 필요시 초음파 처리하여 녹인다.
위 용액 2 mL를 50 mL 용량플라스크에 넣고 희석액으로 표선하고 잘 섞는다.
아질사르탄 표준원액 10 mL를 50 mL 용량플라스크에 넣고 희석액으로 표선하고 잘 섞는다.

암로디핀 20 mg에 해당하는 가루(암로디핀 + 플라시보)의 무게를 달아 200 mL 용량플라스크에 넣는다.
희석액 140 mL를 추가하고 중간에 흔들어주며 20 분 동안 초음파 처리하고 희석액으로 표선하고 잘 섞는다.
이 용액을 5000 RPM에서 10분간 원심분리한다.

20개 이상의 정제의 무게를 측정하고, 유발에 넣고 갈아 가루로 한다.
암로디핀 25 mg에 해당하는 정제 분말을 정확하게 달아 100 mL 용량플라스크에 넣는다.
희석액 70 mL를 추가하고 중간에 흔들어주며 20 분 동안 초음파 처리한다. 플라스크를 실온으로 식히고 희석액으로 표선하고 잘 섞는다.
검액 원액을 5000 RPM에서 10분간 원심분리한다.

26 밀리그램/5 밀리그램 및 52 밀리그램/10 밀리그램
검액 원액 10 mL를 50 mL 용량플라스크에 넣고 희석액으로 표선하고 잘 섞는다.
26 밀리그램/10밀리그램
검액 원액 20 mL를 50 mL 용량플라스크에 넣고 희석액으로 표선하고 잘 섞는다.
52 밀리그램/5밀리그램
검액 원액 5 mL를 50 mL 용량플라스크에 넣고 희석액으로 표선하고 잘 섞는다.
크로마토그래픽 시스템
Note: 분석 후 칼럼을 50 % 아세토니트릴로 2시간동안 세척한다.
절차
아래 설명된 대로 분석을 시작한다.
안정화를 위해 2 회 이상 공시험액을 측정할 수 있다.
시스템 적합성
표준액 3회 반복 주입 시 아질사르탄 피크의 상대표준편차 (%RSD)는 10.0 % 이하이다.
표준액 3회 반복 주입 및 브라켓팅(표준액)에서 얻은 아질사르탄 피크의 상대표준편차(%RSD)는 10.0 % 이하이다.
표준액 3회 반복 주입 시 아질사르탄 피크의 대칭계수는 2.0 이하이다.
계산
아질사르탄
Note: 공시험액 및 플라시보 용액-B 관련 피크는 무시한다.
Note: 아질사르탄 표준품으로 아질사르탄 관련 유연물질을 계산한다.
Note: 약 7분대에 검출되는 Benzenesulfonic acid(Besylate)피크는 무시한다
ASTD	: 표준액 중 아질사르탄 평균 피크면적
ASAM	: 검액 중 아질사르탄 유연물질 피크면적
WSTD	: 취한 아질사르탄 표준품의 양 (mg)
WSAM	: 취한 검체의 양 (mg)
VSAM	: 취한 검액 원액의 부피 (mL)
(26 밀리그램/5 밀리그램 및 52 밀리그램/10 밀리그램: 10 mL,
26 밀리그램/10 밀리그램: 20 mL,
52 밀리그램/5 밀리그램: 5 mL)
P	        : 아질사르탄 표준품의 함량 (%)
LC	: 1 정 당 아질사르탄 표시량 (mg)
AW	: 정제의 평균 질량 (mg)
RRF	: 상대 반응 인자
유연물질 정보
HPLC 크로마토그램
유연물질
방법 Ⅱ (암로디핀)
Note: 완충액, 이동상, 희석액, 용액 안정성은 방법 I과 동일하게 진행한다.
Note: HPLC 등급의 용매 및 고무 바이알 마개를 사용한다.
Note: 메탄올로 바늘 세척 절차를 수행하고 저농도 순서로 검액을 주입하는 것이 권고된다.
Note: 분석 전, 바늘을 메탄올로 충분히 수동세척하고 이동상으로 1시간 이상 칼럼을 안정화한다.
Note: 시험 수행 전 초자와 소니케이터를 정제수로 채우고 약 10분간 초음파 처리한다. 초음파 처리한 초자를 초자세척기로 세척한 후 시험에 사용한다.
Note: 이동상을 멤브레인 필터로 여과하고 탈기한다.
표준품

암로디핀베실산염 표준품 약 34.75 mg을 정밀하게 달아 100 mL 용량플라스크에 넣는다.
희석액으로 녹이고 표선한 후 잘 섞는다. 필요시 초음파 처리하여 녹인다.
위 용액 5 mL를 100 mL 용량플라스크에 넣고 희석액으로 표선하고 잘 섞는다.
암로디핀베실산염 표준원액 5 mL를 50 mL 용량플라스크에 넣고 희석액으로 표선하고 잘 섞는다.

아질사르탄 130 mg에 해당하는 가루(아질사르탄 + 플라시보)의 무게를 달아 50 mL 용량플라스크에 넣는다.
희석액 35 mL를 추가하고 중간에 흔들어주며 20 분 동안 초음파 처리하고 희석액으로 표선하고 잘 섞는다.
이 용액을 5000 RPM에서 10분간 원심분리한다.

20개 이상의 정제의 무게를 측정하고, 유발에 넣고 갈아 가루로 한다.
암로디핀 25 mg에 해당하는 정제 분말을 정확하게 달아 100 mL 용량플라스크에 넣는다.
희석액 70 mL를 추가하고 중간에 흔들어주며 20 분 동안 초음파 처리한다. 플라스크를 실온으로 식히고 희석액으로 표선하고 잘 섞는다. (암로디핀으로써 0.25 mg/mL)
검액 원액을 5000 RPM에서 10분간 원심분리한다.

검액 원액을 그대로 사용한다.
크로마토그래픽 시스템
Note: 분석 후 칼럼을 50 % 아세토니트릴로 2시간동안 세척한다.
절차
아래 설명된 대로 분석을 시작한다.
안정화를 위해 2 회 이상 공시험액을 측정할 수 있다.
시스템 적합성
표준액 3회 반복 주입 시 암로디핀 피크의 상대표준편차 (%RSD)는 10.0 % 이하이다.
표준액 3회 반복 주입 및 브라켓팅(표준액)에서 얻은 암로디핀 피크의 상대표준편차(%RSD)는 10.0 % 이하이다.
표준액 3회 반복 주입 시 암로디핀 피크의 대칭계수는 2.0 이하이다.
계산
암로디핀
Note: 공시험액 및 플라시보 용액-A 관련 피크는 무시한다.
Note: 암로디핀 표준품으로 암로디핀 관련 유연물질을 계산한다.
Note: 검액의 carryover로 인해 브라켓팅 표준액에서 아질사르탄 피크가 검출될 수 있다. 이 경우, 아질사르탄 피크는 무시한다.
Note: 약 7분대에 검출되는 Benzenesulfonic acid(Besylate)피크는 무시한다
ASTD	: 표준액 중 암로디핀 평균 피크면적
ASAM	: 검액 중 암로디핀베실산염 유연물질 피크면적
WSTD	: 취한 암로디핀베실산염 표준품의 양 (mg)
WSAM	: 취한 검체의 양 (mg)
P	        : 암로디핀베실산염 표준품의 함량 (%)
LC	: 1 정 당 암로디핀 표시량 (mg)
AW	: 정제의 평균 질량 (mg)
RRF	: 상대 반응 인자
408.88	: 암로디핀 분자량
567.05	: 암로디핀베실산염 분자량

hplc_conditions.column_spec: Hypersil GOLD, 4.6 x 250 mm, 3µm

preparations.preparation_text: Dissolve about 6.81 g of monobasic potassium phosphate and about 0.9 g of sodium hydroxide in 1000 mL of purified water (pH 6.8 ± 0.05).

Weight and transfer 5.8 g of Ammonium dihydrogen phosphate in 1000 mL of Milli-Q water.
Mix above solution, acetonitrile in the ratio of 600:400 v/v.
Add about 4.3 g of sodium dodecyl sulfate. Filter through 0.45 µm membrane filter. Degas in a sonicator for about 10 minutes.
Note: Use a 0.45 µm RC membrane filter.

Weigh and transfer about 37.8 mg of Alogliptin benzoate standard into a 100 mL volumetric flask.
Add about 50 mL of Dissolution medium and sonicate for about 5 minutes and dilute to volume with dissolution media and mix well.

Weigh and transfer about 111.1 mg of Metformin HCl standard into a 100 mL volumetric flask.
Add about 50 mL of Dissolution medium and sonicate for about 5 minutes.
Pipette 5 mL of standard stock solution into a 100 mL volumetric flask and dilute to volume with dissolution media and mix well.

Weigh and transfer about 111.1 mg of Metformin HCl standard into a 100 mL volumetric flask.
Add about 50 mL of Dissolution medium and sonicate for about 5 minutes.
Pipette 10 mL of standard stock solution into a 100 mL volumetric flask and dilute to volume with dissolution media and mix well.

Weigh and transfer about 55.6 mg of Metformin HCl standard into a 100 mL volumetric flask.
Add about 50 mL of Dissolution medium and sonicate for about 5 minutes.
Pipette 5 mL of standard stock solution into a 100 mL volumetric flask and dilute to volume with dissolution media and mix well.

Transfer 900 mL dissolution medium into each dissolution bowl. Drop one tablet into each bowl using a sinker, run the dissolution as per the dissolution parameters.
Note: Apply the autosampler parameter of 3.1.10. if using Autosampler.
Withdraw 10 mL of sample aliquot from each bowl at specified time.
Filter the sample solution through 10 µm Auto sampler filter (or) 0.45 µm PVDF (Millipore) syringe filter.
Dissolution Parameters
Autosampler parameter
Note: Apply the parameter if dissolution through the autosampler.
Chromatographic System
Procedure
Inject the following solutions as mentioned below.
Blank solution can be injected two or more for stabilization.
System Suitability
The relative standard deviation for peak areas of Alogliptin from the replicate standard injections should not be more than 2.0 %.
The tailing factor for Alogliptin in standard should not be more than 2.0.
The relative standard deviation for peak areas of Alogliptin from the replicate standard injections and bracketing standard should not be more than 2.0 %.
Calculation
AS	: Average peak area of Alogliptin peak in the standard solution
AT	: Peak area of Alogliptin peak in the sample solution
Ws	: Weight of standard taken in mg as Alogliptin benzoate
V	: Volume of standard stock solution taken (mL).
P	: Potency of the standard as Alogliptin benzoate, on as is basis
LC	: Label claim of the tablets in mg as Alogliptin.
339.39	: Molar weight of Alogliptin.
461.51	: Molar weight of Alogliptin benzoate.
Note: Continue testing through the three levels unless the results confirm at either S1 or S2.
*Q: Quantity, the amount of dissolved active ingredient specified in the individual monograph, expressed as a percentage of the labeled content.
Chromatogram
Method II: Metformin HCl
Note: Test Information, Preparation of Dissolution medium, Mobile phase, Standard stock solution, Standard solution and Procedure are same as Method I.
Note: Use all HPLC grade solvents and rubber vial cap.
Note: Before analysis, equilibrate the column for at least 2 hours in the mobile phase.
Note: Use Wako’s Sodium dodecyl sulfate.
Note: It is highly recommended to wash using 10% ACN after analysis.

Transfer 900 mL dissolution medium into each dissolution bowl. Drop one tablet into each bowl using a sinker, run the dissolution as per the dissolution parameters.
Note: Apply the autosampler parameter of 3.2.3. if using Autosampler.
Withdraw 10 mL of sample aliquot from each bowl at specified time.
Replace with the same volume of the dissolution medium maintained at 37°C at each specified time interval.
Filter the sample solution through 10 µm Auto sampler filter (or) 0.45 µm PVDF (Millipore) syringe filter.
Dissolution Parameters
1) When Dissolution is performed together with the Dissolution Method I: Alogliptin benzoate, Proceed this sampling.
Autosampler parameter
Note: Apply the parameter if dissolution through the autosampler.
Chromatographic System
System Suitability
The relative standard deviation for peak areas of Metformin HCl from the replicate standard injections should not be more than 2.0 %.
The tailing factor for Metformin HCl in standard should not be more than 2.5.
The relative standard deviation for peak areas of Metformin HCl from the replicate standard injections and bracketing standard should not be more than 2.0 %.
Calculation
AS	: Average peak area of Metformin HCl peak in the standard solution
AT	: Peak area of Metformin HCl peak in the sample solution
Ws	: Weight of standard taken in mg as Metformin HCl
P	: Potency of the standard as Metformin HCl, on as is basis
LC	: Label claim of the tablets in mg as Metformin HCl
Calculation of Correction factors
Note: Here “V” is sampled volume and “D” is dissolution rate.
% of Metformin HCl present in sampled volume after 1st time interval, F1 = (D1 / 900) x V
% of Metformin HCl present in sampled volume after 2st time interval, F2 = (D2 / 900) x V
% of Metformin HCl present in sampled volume after nth time interval, Fn = (Dn / 900) x V
Calculation of Corrected results
For 1st time interval   = D1 (No correction required)
For 2nd time interval   = D2 + F1
For nth time interval   = Dn + F1 + …………….. Fn-1
Note: Continue testing through the three levels unless the results confirm at either L1 or L2.
Chromatogram
Weigh and transfer about 3.4 g of potassium phosphate monobasic in 1000 mL of Milli-Q water. Adjust the pH to 3.0 ± 0.02 with phosphoric acid.
Mix the Buffer : ACN = 900:100 v/v respectively. Sonicate for 10minutes.
Mix the Buffer : ACN = 400:600 v/v respectively. Sonicate for 10minutes.
Use mobile phase-A.

Weigh and transfer about 42.5 mg of Alogliptin benzoate reference standard or working standard into a 50 mL volumetric flask.
Add about 30 mL of Diluent, sonicate for about 5 minutes to dissolve, and dilute to volume with Diluent.
Pipette 5 mL of above solution into a 25 mL volumetric flask and dilute to volume with Diluent.
Pipette 5 mL of above solution into a 20 mL volumetric flask and dilute to volume with Diluent.

preparations.section_name: Sample solution preparation (12.5/1000 mg and 12.5/500 mg)
preparations.preparation_text:
Note: Before prepare test solution, wash magnetic bar well.
Weigh and transfer about 1 tablet into a 100 mL volumetric flask.
Add about 60 mL of Diluent and stir using about 2 cm of magnetic bar for 1hour.
Note: After adding diluent into volumetric flask, stir using magnetic bar immediately to avoid tablet sticking to the volumetric flask.
Note: After starting the stirring using magnetic bar, carefully check that the tablets should not stick with flask.
Sonicate for 5 minutes and remove magnetic bar and then wash the magnetic bar with diluent. Then dilute to volume with diluent and mix well.
Pipette 5 mL of the above solution into a 20 mL volumetric flask and then dilute to volume with diluent and mix well.
Centrifuge at 5000 rpm for 10 min or filter through 0.45 µm PVDF (Milliopore) syringe filter or filter through 0.45 µm PTFE (Millipore) syringe filter or filter through 0.45 µm Nylon (Millipore) syringe filter.

Note: Before prepare test solution, wash magnetic bar well.
Weigh and transfer about 1 tablet into a 100 mL volumetric flask.
Add about 60 mL of Diluent and stir using about 2 cm of magnetic bar for 1hour.
Note: After adding diluent into volumetric flask, stir using magnetic bar immediately to avoid tablet sticking to the volumetric flask.
Note: After starting the stirring using magnetic bar, carefully check that the tablets should not stick with flask.
Sonicate for 5 minutes and remove magnetic bar and then wash the magnetic bar with diluent. Then dilute to volume with diluent and mix well.
Pipette 3 mL of the above solution into a 25 mL volumetric flask and then dilute to volume with diluent and mix well.
Centrifuge at 5000 rpm for 10 min or filter through 0.45 µm PVDF (Milliopore) syringe filter or filter through 0.45 µm PTFE (Millipore) syringe filter or filter through 0.45 µm Nylon (Millipore) syringe filter.
Chromatographic System
Procedure
Inject the following solutions as mentioned below.
Blank solution can be injected two or more for stabilization.
System Suitability
The relative standard deviation for peak areas of Alogliptin from the replicate standard injections should not be more than 2.0 %.
The tailing factor for Alogliptin in standard should not be more than 2.0.
The relative standard deviation for peak areas of Alogliptin from the replicate standard injections and bracketing standard should not be more than 2.0 %.
Calculation
AS	: Average peak area of Alogliptin peak in the standard solution
AT	: Peak area of Alogliptin peak in the sample solution
Ws	: Weight of standard taken in mg as Alogliptin benzoate
V1	: Volume of Sample solution (mL)
V2	: Volume of sample stock solution taken (mL)
P	: Potency of the standard as Alogliptin benzoate, on as is basis
LC	: Label claim of the tablets in mg as Alogliptin
339.39	: Molar Weight of Alogliptin.
461.51	: Molar Weight of Alogliptin Benzoate.
Acceptance value (AV)
Calculate the Acceptance value as follows:
Acceptance value (AV) = | M –  | + ks
The requirements for dosage uniformity are met if the acceptance value of the first 10 dosage units is less than or equal to L1 %, maximum allowed acceptance value
If the acceptance value is > L1 %, test additional 20 units and calculate the acceptance value. The requirements are met if the final acceptance value of the 30 dosage units is ≤ L1 %, and no individual content of any dosage units is less than [1-(0.01)(L2)]M nor more than [1+(0.01)(L2)]M as specified in the calculation of Acceptance Value under Content Uniformity or weight variation. Unless otherwise specified in L1 is 15.0 and L2 is 25.0.
Weight variation (For Metformin HCl)
Note: Acceptance value (AV) follows 4.1. Content uniformity (For Alogliptin benzoate)
Procedure
Accurately weigh 10 tablets individually.
Calculate the estimated contents of each tablet from the weight of the individual tablet and the result of the Assay.
Calculate the acceptance value (AV).
Calculation
WSAM 	: Individual weights of the units tested
A 	: Content of drug substance (% of label claim)
W 	: Mean of individual weights

Weigh and transfer about 1.2 g of Ammonium dihydrogen phosphate and 1.8 g of 1-sodium pentane sulfonate in 1000 mL of Milli-Q water.
Stir 1 hour and sonicate 10 minutes.
Filter through 0.45 µm membrane filter.
Degas in a sonicator for about 10 minutes.

Mix the Milli-Q water : ACN = 300 : 700 v/v respectively.
Degas in a sonicator for about 10 minutes.
Transfer 8.5 mL of Hydrochloric acid to a suitable container, dissolve and dilute in 1000 mL of Milli-Q water, mix well.

Weigh and transfer about 50 mg of Metformin HCl reference standard or working standard into a 100 mL volumetric flask.
Add about 50 mL of Diluent, sonicate for about 5 minutes to dissolve, and dilute to volume with Diluent.
Pipette 5 mL of above solution into a 50 mL volumetric flask and dilute to volume with Diluent.

Note: Before preparation, Wash magnetic bar well.
Weigh and Crush not less than 20 tablets to a fine powder (Using automatic mixer. Wait for 10 minutes to settle down a fine powder). Mix well thoroughly for 30 seconds with spatula. The powder is weighed within 30 minutes.
Accurately weigh and transfer portion of tablet powder equivalent to 250 mg of Metformin HCl into 500 mL volumetric flask and spread the power evenly.
Measure 100 mL of diluent and then wash the weighing paper with little amount of diluent and add remaining diluent to flask while shake flask with hand.
Add 3.8 cm of magnetic bar and then add 300 mL of diluent to the flask while stand flask upright.
Stir immediately at 1400 RPM for 1 hour using a multi-magnetic stirrer. Often check if the magnetic bar doesn’t stir.
Drop roundly 1 mL of ACN through the volumetric flask wall to remove the foam. And then check whether there are floating gels or not. If there’re floating gels, additionally stir until the gels disappears and do remove foam like above procedure once again.
And then, sonicate immediately for about 30 minutes with intermediate shaking at least per every 5 minutes.
Add 90 mL of diluent into the flask and remove the magnetic bar at the same time. When remove magnetic bar, wash it with diluent.
Add 0.5 mL of ACN drop slowly to remove the foam and then dilute to volume with diluent and mix well.
Centrifuge the sample solution at 5000 rpm for 10 minutes, or filter through 0.45 µm PVDF (Millipore) syringe filter.
Pipette 5 mL of the above solution into a 50 mL volumetric flask and then dilute to volume with diluent and mix well.

Note: This item is limited to outsourced testing conducted exclusively for the Korean market.
Dissolve NDMA standard solution in methanol to achieve a concentration of 1 μg/mL.
Mix the standard solution and internal standard solution in the amounts specified in the table below and dilute to 10 mL with dichloromethane accurately.

Accurately weigh not less than 20 tablets and finely powder. Accurately weigh equivalent to 500 mg of Metformin Hydrochloride, add 5 mL of 1 mol/L hydrochloric acid, then accurately take 50 μL of the internal standard solution and shake thoroughly to mix.
Add 5 mL of dichloromethane to this solution accurately, shake thoroughly to mix, and then centrifuge at 4,000 rpm for 10 minutes.1) Carefully take an appropriate amount of the dichloromethane layer, filter it through a 0.22 μm membrane filter, discard the first filtrate, and use the subsequent filtrate as the sample solution.

System suitability
The coefficient of determination (R2) of the calibration curve should be not less than 0.998.
The S/N ratio at a standard solution concentration of 1 ng/mL should be not less than 10.
The tailing factor of NDMA peak at a standard solution concentration of 5 ng/mL should be not more than 2.0.
The relative standard deviation of the NDMA peak area to the internal standard peak area for standard solution concentration of 5 ng/mL five replicate injection should be not more than 5 %.
Loss on drying (LOD)
Note: After grinding the tablets, weight it and transfer to a desiccator within 30 minutes and put it in a drying chamber. (To prevent unnecessary absorption)
Oven condition
Procedure
Accurately weigh an appropriate glass-stoppered, shallow weighing bottle that has been dried for about 30 minutes under the same condition to be employed in the determination and cool to room temperature in a desiccator.
Crush not less than 20 tablets to a fine powder using automatic mixer.
Accurately weigh and transfer about 2 g of tablet powder into a weighing bottle.
Replace the cover, distribute the test specimen by gentle sidewise shaking. Place the loaded bottle in the drying chamber, remove the stopper and leave it also in the chamber. (When moving to the drying chamber, put it the desiccator and transport it)
Dry the sample at the temperature and for the specified time.
After drying, upon opening the drying chamber, close the bottle promptly, and allow it to come to room temperature in a desiccator before weighing. Accurately weigh the bottle with the contents.
Calculation
Calculate the loss on drying of the specimen, in % w/w, taken by the formula.
WB	: Mass, in g, of the blank weighing stoppered bottle
WT	: Mass, in g, of the test powder
WS	: Mass, in g, of the weighing stoppered bottle with test powder after drying.

Blank solution can be injected more for stabilization.
System Suitability
The resolution between Valsartan and SCB4-Impurity 1 should not be less than 2.0 from system suitability solution.
The relative standard deviation for peak areas of Sacubitril and Valsartan from the replicated standard injections should not be more than 10.0 %.
The relative standard deviation for peak areas of Sacubitril and Valsartan from the replicate standard injections and bracketing should not be more than 10.0 %.
Detailed Label Claim
Relative retention time information about Sacubitril related impurities

Valsartan related impurities
AS	: Average peak area of Valsartan from the standard solution
AT	: Peak area of Valsartan impurity from the sample solution
Ws	: Weight of Sacubitril valsartan sodium standard taken in mg.
WT	: Weight of sample powder taken in mg.
Aw	: Average weight of the tablet in mg.
P	: Potency of the standard as Valsartan, on as is basis
L	: Label claim of the tablets in mg as Valsartan
Sacubitril related impurities
AS	: Average peak area of Sacubitril from the standard solution
AT	: Peak area of Sacubitril impurity from the sample solution
Ws	: Weight of Sacubitril valsartan sodium standard taken in mg.
WT	: Weight of sample powder taken in mg.
Aw	: Average weight of the tablet in mg.
P	: Potency of the standard as Sacubitril, on as is basis
L	: Label claim of the tablets in mg as Sacubitril
RRF	: Relative response factor. (Unknown impurity is ‘1’)

Microbial Enumeration Test (Only for EU market)
Diluent and Media
Test Method
Note: Perform culture detection test using pour-plate method.
Put 10 g of sample into 90 mL of diluent and mix it to homogenize.

Add 20 mL of the sample solution (Dilution factor 1:10) to 80 mL of diluent. And shake it to suspension.
Total Aerobic Microbial Count (TAMC) Test
Place 2 petri dishes and transfer 1 mL of sample solution (Dilution factor 1:50) into each petri dish.
Pour 15 ~ 20 mL of TSA to each petri dish with sample solution dispersed. Swirl the petri dishes slowly to mix sample with media.
Incubate at 30 - 35 ˚C for 5 days.
Perform interim colony counting on days 2-4 and final colony counting on days 5 of incubation.
Note: It can be possible to incubate additionally 2 days (Total 7 days).
After incubation, resulting value is calculated by averaging the values obtained by multiplying dilution rate 50 to colony number detected from each petri dish.
Total Combined Yeasts and Molds Count (TYMC) Test
Place 2 petri dishes and transfer 1 mL of sample solution (Dilution factor 1:10) into each petri dish.
Pour 15 ~ 20 mL of SDA to each petri dish with sample solution dispersed. Swirl the petri dishes slowly to mix sample with media.
Incubate at 20 - 25 ˚C for 7 days.
Perform Interim colony counting on days 3-5 and final colony counting on days 7 of incubation.
After incubation, resulting value is calculated by averaging the values obtained by multiplying dilution rate 10 to colony number detected from each petri dish.
Negative Control Test
Perform tests using diluent without sample in the same as the above procedure.
Specified Microorganisms Test: Escherichia coli (E.coli) (Only for EU market)
Media
Test Method
Note: Test method is selection culture method of E. coli using selective media (MCB) and confirmatory medium (MCA).

Note: Sample solution (Dilution factor 1:10) of microbial enumeration test that is the same sample solution method can be used.
Put 10 g of sample into 90 mL of diluent and mix it to homogenize.
Pre-incubation
Transfer 10 mL of sample solution into the 90 mL of TSB. After that, mix the sample to homogenize.
Incubate at 30-35 ℃ for 18-24 hours.
Selection and Subculture
Shake the container, and transfer 1 mL of incubated TSB to 100 mL MCB.
Incubate at 42-44 ℃ for 24-48 hours.
Streak incubated MCB on the surface of MCA using inoculating loop.
Incubate at 30-35 ℃ for 18-72 hours.
Note: It can be possible to incubate additionally 48 hours (Total 120 hours).
After incubation, confirm the growth of colonies on the surface of MCA.
Growth of colonies indicates the possible presence of E.coli, This is confirmed by identification tests, and follow the QS09004 Microbial Impact Assessment Procedure to determine whether MIA is required.
Negative Control Test
Perform tests without sample in the same as the above procedure.
